FHA Down Payment Assistance Florida

FHA down payment assistance in Florida

Short on cash to close? Florida down payment assistance programs can pair with your FHA loan to cover your down payment and closing costs — here's how it works.

Down Payment Help

FHA down payment assistance in Florida

One of the biggest hurdles to buying a home is the down payment and closing costs — not the monthly payment. Florida offers several down payment assistance (DPA) programs that can be paired with an FHA loan to bridge that gap, sometimes covering most or all of your upfront cash.

Common Florida assistance options

Programs available to Florida buyers have historically included the Florida Hometown Heroes program (aimed at eligible workforce buyers), Florida Assist and other Florida Housing second-mortgage options, and local SHIP (State Housing Initiatives Partnership) programs run at the county and city level. Many are structured as low- or zero-interest second mortgages or deferred loans used for down payment and closing costs.

How DPA works with an FHA loan

Your FHA first mortgage covers most of the purchase, and the assistance program supplies funds for your 3.5% down payment and/or closing costs. You still go through normal FHA qualifying — credit, income, and the property requirements all apply.

Eligibility basics

Most programs have income limits, require the home to be your primary residence, and ask that you complete a homebuyer education course. First-time buyer status is required for some programs but not all. Because funding and rules change frequently, the current details should always be confirmed before you rely on them.

Program names, eligibility, and funding availability change often and can pause when funds run out. Always confirm current Florida Housing and local SHIP program details before counting on assistance — we can check what's currently available for your situation.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

Can I use down payment assistance with an FHA loan in Florida?+
Yes. FHA loans are commonly paired with Florida down payment assistance programs, which can supply funds for your 3.5% down payment and/or closing costs.
What down payment assistance programs are available in Florida?+
Options have historically included Florida Hometown Heroes, Florida Assist and other Florida Housing second mortgages, and local SHIP programs. Availability and funding change, so current details should be confirmed.
Do I need to be a first-time buyer for Florida DPA?+
Some programs require first-time buyer status; others don't. Many define a first-time buyer as someone who hasn't owned a home in the past three years.
Are there income limits for Florida down payment assistance?+
Yes, most programs have household income limits that vary by county and program, along with a homebuyer education requirement.
Does down payment assistance have to be repaid?+
It depends on the program — some are deferred or forgivable second mortgages, others are repaid over time. We can explain the terms of any program you're considering.
